user_query:
Please run a budget vs. actual variance analysis for March 2026 using the files under input/ and produce the specified outputs under output/.

Inputs to read:
- input/budget.csv — monthly budget by account for Jan–Mar 2026 (columns: date,account,amount)
- input/actuals.csv — monthly actuals by account for Jan–Mar 2026 (columns: date,account,amount)
- input/mapping.csv — chart-of-accounts mapping (columns: raw_account,line_item,section,subcategory,kind) where kind is "revenue" or "expense"
- input/period.yaml — analysis period and materiality thresholds (keys: period: "2026-03", period_type: "month", ytd_start: "2026-01", materiality.revenue.amount, materiality.revenue.pct, materiality.expense.amount, materiality.expense.pct, materiality.ebitda.amount, materiality.ebitda.pct)

What to do:
1) Normalize budget and actuals to the standardized P&L line items using input/mapping.csv. Aggregate multiple raw accounts into the mapped line_item if applicable.
2) Build a March 2026 (period = 2026-03) variance table at the line_item level and also compute derived totals: Total Revenue, COGS, Gross Profit, Total OpEx, and EBITDA (Gross Profit − Total OpEx). Use these formulas:
   - variance_abs = actual − budget
   - variance_pct = (actual − budget) / abs(budget) × 100, rounded to 2 decimals; if budget = 0, write N/A (exactly that string)
   - favorable (boolean): for kind = revenue, favorable if actual > budget; for kind = expense, favorable if actual < budget
   - material (boolean): for line items of kind = revenue, true if |variance_abs| ≥ materiality.revenue.amount OR |variance_pct| ≥ materiality.revenue.pct; for kind = expense, true if |variance_abs| ≥ materiality.expense.amount OR |variance_pct| ≥ materiality.expense.pct; for EBITDA, apply materiality.ebitda thresholds
3) Build a YTD variance table from 2026-01 through 2026-03 with the same columns and logic (sum budget and actuals across the YTD window before calculating variances). Include the same derived totals (Total Revenue, COGS, Gross Profit, Total OpEx, EBITDA).
4) Produce a structured JSON file containing both monthly and YTD summaries and line items.

Required outputs to write:
- output/march_variance.csv — columns exactly: line_item,section,subcategory,kind,budget,actual,variance_abs,variance_pct,favorable,material; rows must include, at minimum, these line items: Product Sales; Service Fees; Direct Materials; Direct Labor; Advertising; Sales Commissions; Engineering Contractors; Software Tools; Legal & Accounting; Rent; S&M - Conferences; Total Revenue; COGS; Gross Profit; Total OpEx; EBITDA.
- output/ytd_variance.csv — same schema and required line items, but for YTD (2026-01 through 2026-03).
- output/bva.json — must include keys: period ("2026-03"), period_type ("month"), materiality (echo thresholds used), summary.monthly (total_revenue_budget, total_revenue_actual, total_revenue_variance_abs, total_revenue_variance_pct, ebitda_budget, ebitda_actual, ebitda_variance_abs, ebitda_variance_pct), summary.ytd (same fields), line_items.monthly (array of objects for each line_item including the derived totals) and line_items.ytd (same), with fields: name, section, subcategory, kind, budget, actual, variance_abs, variance_pct (or "N/A"), favorable (boolean), material (boolean).

Rules and expectations:
- Use only the provided input files. Do not invent or drop any mapping.
- For missing budget rows that exist in actuals (e.g., unbudgeted items), treat budget as 0, set variance_pct to N/A, and evaluate materiality using only the dollar threshold for that line’s kind.
- Round all monetary values in outputs to the nearest integer (no decimals). Round variance_pct to 2 decimals.
- Ensure totals are consistent: Total Revenue = sum of revenue line_items; COGS = sum of COGS line_items; Gross Profit = Total Revenue − COGS; Total OpEx = sum of OpEx line_items; EBITDA = Gross Profit − Total OpEx.
- Ensure favorable/material booleans match the rules above.

Write the three files to the output/ directory with the exact filenames shown.
